\makeatletter
\def\@nodottedtocline#1#2#3#4#5{%
    \vskip \z@ \@plus.2\p@
    {\leftskip #2\relax \rightskip \@tocrmarg \parfillskip -\rightskip
     \parindent #2\relax\@afterindenttrue
     \interlinepenalty\@M
     \leavevmode
     \@tempdima #3\relax
     \advance\leftskip \@tempdima \null\nobreak\hskip -\leftskip
     {#4}\hfill\null\par}}
\newcommand*\l@heading{\@nodottedtocline{0}{0em}{0em}}
\makeatother

  \addtocontents{lot}{\vspace{5pt}}}

\multicolumn{4}{|l}{13.14 BUS\_REMAP5} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40000034}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 12 & \footnotesize rf\_blk5\_remap\-\_offset & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0xFC000 & \footnotesize  block 5 remap offset when the block 5 remap function is enabled. \newline
The address after remap will be original addr + rf\_blk5\_remap\_offset \\
\hline
\end{longtable}

The address after remap will be original addr + rf\_blk6\_remap\_offset \\
\hline
\end{longtable}

\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{13.16 BUS\_REMAP7}

The address after remap will be original addr + rf\_blk7\_remap\_offset \\

\pdfbookmark[2]{13.17 CACHE\_CFG0}{anchor28}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{13.17 CACHE\_CFG0}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{13.17 CACHE\_CFG0} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40000040}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 : 0 & \footnotesize rf\_write\_mode & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  cache write operation mode: \newline
2’b00: write through\newline
2’b01: write back, no write allocate\newline
2’b10: write back, write allocate \\
\hline
\footnotesize 5 : 2 & \footnotesize rf\_hprot\_byp & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Bit [5]: \newline
1’b1: hprot[3] from CM4 go through cache  controller without modification\newline
1’b0: hprot[3] is provided by cache controller register\newline
Bit [4]: \newline
1’b1: hprot[2] from CM4 go through cache controller without modification\newline
1’b0: hprot[2] is provided by cache controller register\newline
Bit [3]: \newline
1’b1: hprot[1] from CM4 go through cache controller without modification\newline
1’b0: hprot[1] is provided by cache controller register\newline
Bit [2]: \newline
1’b1: hprot[0] from CM4 go through cache controller without modification\newline
1’b0: hprot[0] is provided by cache controller register \\
\hline
\footnotesize 9 : 6 & \footnotesize rf\_hprot\_bus\_\-reg & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x3 & \footnotesize  hprot control register which provide 4bit hprot for cache bus AHB \\
\hline
\footnotesize 13 : 10 & \footnotesize rf\_hprot\_cach\-e\_reg & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0xB & \footnotesize  hprot control register which provide 4bit hprot for cache ctrl AHB \\
\hline
\footnotesize 14 & \footnotesize ahb\_ram\_en & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Cache Data Ram Can be Write/Read Throught rf AHB Bus \\
\hline
\footnotesize 29 : 28 & \footnotesize rf\_cache\_size\-\_sel & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x3 & \footnotesize  cache size selection: \newline
0: 4K Byte\newline
1: 8K Byte\newline
2: 16K Byte\newline
3: 32K Byte \\
\hline
\footnotesize 30 & \footnotesize rf\_cmd\_exec\_\-mode & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  0: (recommended) software can run in cacheable region during software command processing\newline
1: software cannot run in cacheable region during software command processing \\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 & \footnotesize rf\_debug\_en & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  cache debug mode enable: \newline
0: normal mode\newline
1: debug mode\newline
This bit MUST always be cleared during cache operating \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{13.18 BUS\_STS0}{anchor29}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{13.18 BUS\_STS0}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{13.18 BUS\_STS0} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40000044}\\

\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 27 : 0 & \footnotesize rf\_prot\_detec\-t\_addr & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  trigging address for protect block \\

\pdfbookmark[2]{13.19 CACHE\_STS0}{anchor30}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{13.19 CACHE\_STS0}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{13.19 CACHE\_STS0} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40000048}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 : 0 & \footnotesize rf\_cache\_st & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  rf\_cache\_st \\
\hline
\footnotesize 6 : 4 & \footnotesize rf\_cmd\_st & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  rf\_cmd\_st \\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 & \footnotesize rf\_write\_ongo\-ing & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  rf\_write\_ongoing, this is a status register for write through mode to avoid potential coherence issue.\newline
1’b1: the cache is still doing AHB write transaction to the main memory and the data is not written into the main memory.\newline
1’b0: the cache has finished AHB write transaction and the data is written into the main memory. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}

\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\

cmd\_range : start address\newline
cmd\_entry  : entry address \\
\hline

\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\

\footnotesize 27 : 0 & \footnotesize rf\_cmd\_end\_a\-ddr & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  cmd\_all      : not used\newline
cmd\_range : end address\newline
cmd\_entry  : not used \\

\pdfbookmark[2]{13.22 CMD\_CFG2}{anchor33}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{13.22 CMD\_CFG2}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{13.22 CMD\_CFG2} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40000058}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 5 : 0 & \footnotesize rf\_cmd\_type & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  software command type:\newline
6’h0 : clean all\newline
6’h1 : clean range\newline
6’h2 : clean entry\newline
6’h3 : reserved\newline
6’h4 : invalid all\newline
6’h5 : invalid range\newline
6’h6 : invalid entry\newline
6’h7 : reserved\newline
6’h8 : clean and invalid all\newline
6’h9 : clean and invalid range\newline
6’hA : clean and invalid entry\newline
6’hB : reserved \\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 & \footnotesize rf\_cmd\_str & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  software command start: \newline
write 1 to this bit to issue one command \\
\hline
\end{longtable}

\headinginfo{dma\_cx}
\section{dma\_cx}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{14.1 DMA\_PAUSE}{anchor44}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{14.1 DMA\_PAUSE}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{14.1 DMA\_PAUSE} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40020000}\\
\hline
\multicolumn{5}{|l|}{Discription: }\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize DMA\_PAUSE & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Active High, pause all the channels.\newline
Note: If "DMA\_PAUSE" asserted active when the DMA  is busy, DMA would finish the current fragment before enter Pause State. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 16 & \footnotesize DMA\_PAUSE\_STA\-TUS & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  DMA Pause status.\newline
0 : In active state.\newline
1 : In Pause state. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}

\hline

\hline
\footnotesize 28 & \footnotesize O\_ENDIAN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  DMA output endian, \newline
0- little endian  \newline
1- big endian \\

\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{14.7 DMA\_REQ\_STATUS} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40020018}\\
\hline
\multicolumn{5}{|l|}{Discription: }\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 0 & \footnotesize DMA\_REQ\_STATU\-S & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  DMA 32 Channels Request Status.\newline
0 : No Request need to be served.\newline
1 : Request need to be served. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{14.8 DMA\_EN\_STATUS}{anchor51}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{14.8 DMA\_EN\_STATUS}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{14.8 DMA\_EN\_STATUS} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x4002001C}\\
\hline
\multicolumn{5}{|l|}{Discription: }\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 0 & \footnotesize DMA\_EN\_STATUS & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  DMA 32 Channels Enable Status.\newline
0 : Channel Not Enabled.\newline
1 : Channel Enabled. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{14.9 DMA\_DEBUG\_STATUS}{anchor52}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{14.9 DMA\_DEBUG\_STATUS}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{14.9 DMA\_DEBUG\_STATUS} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40020020}\\
\hline
\multicolumn{5}{|l|}{Discription: }\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 : 0 & \footnotesize DEST\_FSM\_STAT\-US & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Destination Side FSM status.\newline
4'd0: IDLE\newline
4'd1: calculate burst parameter\newline
4'd2: calculate data number of burst\newline
4'd3: wait enough data buffer space for write  burst\newline
4'd4: start write burst\newline
4'd5: write burst execute\newline
4'd6: wait fragment done or limit outstanding  number\newline
4'd7: fragment write done \\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 : 4 & \footnotesize SRC\_FSM\_STATU\-S & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Source Side FSM status.\newline
4'd0: IDLE\newline
4'd1: calculate burst parameter\newline
4'd2: calculate data number of burst\newline
4'd3: wait enough data buffer space for read  burst\newline
4'd4: start read burst\newline
4'd5: read burst execute\newline
4'd6: wait fragment done or limit outstanding  number\newline
4'd7: fragment read done \\
\hline
\footnotesize 12 : 8 & \footnotesize MAIN\_FSM\_STAT\-US & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  MAIN FSM status.\newline
5'd0: IDLE\newline
5'd1: load link list pointer\newline
5'd2: load link list source and dest address\newline
5'd3: load link list fragment and block length\newline
5'd4: load link list transaction length and  src/dest transfer step\newline
5'd5: load link list wrap\_ptr and wrap\_to\newline
5'd6: load full mode source and dest address\newline
5'd7: load full mode fragment and block length\newline
5'd8: load full mode transaction length and  src/dest transfer step\newline
5'd9: load full mode wrap\_ptr and wrap\_to\newline
5'd10: update block length\newline
5'd11: update transaction length\newline
5'd12: fragment execute\newline
5'd13: load list pointer and use src/dest fragment  step\newline
5'd14: load src/dest block step\newline
5'd15: load link list high 4bits pointer and  src/dest fragment step\newline
5'd16: load src/dest block step\newline
5'd17: transaction or link list done\newline
5'd18: update wrap info. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 16 & \footnotesize HREADY\_DMAR & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  dma read hready \\
\hline
\footnotesize 17 & \footnotesize HREADY\_DMAW & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  dma write hready \\
\hline
\footnotesize 20 & \footnotesize DMA\_BUSY & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  DMA Busy Status. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}

\hline
\footnotesize 3 : 0 & \footnotesize DATA\_MODE & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0xE & \footnotesize  CRC data mode\newline
bit0: In data byte receive\newline
bit1: In data bit receive\newline
bit2: Out data byte receive\newline
bit3: Out data bit receive \\
\hline
\footnotesize 5 : 4 & \footnotesize CRC\_GEN\_SIZE & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x3 & \footnotesize  CRC generator size \newline
2'b0:crc8 2'b1:crc16 \newline
2'b10:crc24 2'b11:crc32 \\
\hline
\footnotesize 9 : 8 & \footnotesize CRC\_INIT\_MODE & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  CRC initial mode\newline
2'b0:frag done 2'b1:blk done 2'b10:trsc done \\
\hline
\footnotesize 20 : 16 & \footnotesize CRC\_CH\_NUM & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  DMA channel number for CRC check \\
\hline
\footnotesize 24 & \footnotesize CRC\_HOLD & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  CRC hold \\
\hline
\footnotesize 28 & \footnotesize CRC\_EN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  CRC enable \\
\hline
\footnotesize 29 & \footnotesize CRC\_CLK\_GATE\_\-EN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  CRC clock gate enable \\
\hline
\end{longtable}

\paragraph{}
\pdfbookmark[3]{14.46.1 FULL\_CHN\_PAUSE}{anchor90}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{14.46.46 FULL\_CHN\_PAUSE}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{14.46.1 FULL\_CHN\_PAUSE} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40021000}\\
\hline
\multicolumn{5}{|l|}{\textbf{Discription} : }\\
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize CHN\_PAUSE & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Channel Pause Enable.\newline
0 : Channel in active mode.\newline
1 : Channel pause.\newline
Active high, only pause the channel. If the  CHN\_PAUSE asserted when the channel is busy, it should be paused after the  current fragment finished. \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 16 & \footnotesize CHN\_PAUSE\_STA\-TUS & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Channel Pause status. \\
\hline                
\end{longtable}
                
\paragraph{}
\pdfbookmark[3]{14.46.2 FULL\_CHN\_REQ}{anchor91}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{14.46.46 FULL\_CHN\_REQ}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{14.46.2 FULL\_CHN\_REQ} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40021004}\\
\hline
\multicolumn{5}{|l|}{\textbf{Discription} : }\\
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize CHN\_REQ & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Channel Software DMA Request. \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize CHN\_EXREQ\_MOD\-E & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Channel External DMA Request Mode: \newline
1- level, 0- edge \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 2 & \footnotesize CHN\_EXREQ\_LEV\-EL & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  Channel External DMA Valid Level: \newline
1- high, 0- low \\
\hline                
\end{longtable}
                
\paragraph{}
\pdfbookmark[3]{14.46.3 FULL\_CHN\_CFG}{anchor92}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{14.46.46 FULL\_CHN\_CFG}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{14.46.3 FULL\_CHN\_CFG} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40021008}\\
\hline
\multicolumn{5}{|l|}{\textbf{Discription} : }\\
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize CHN\_EN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Channel Enable:\newline
0 : Disable the channel.\newline
1 : Enable the channel. \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize LLIST\_EN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Link list Enable, only available for full DMA  channel.\newline
0 : Disable the link list.\newline
1 : Enable the link list. \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 8 & \footnotesize LLIST\_CFG\_VAL\-ID & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  If "LLIST\_EN" asserted, active high indicates  Link list configure valid.\newline
0 : Link list configure in-valid. DMA controller  need obtain link list configure information by link list pointer.\newline
1 : link list configure information valid. DMA  controller would continue to complete the node and clear this bit.\newline
Note: Strongly recommend to observe only.  Carefully writing to this bit, which may cause DMA crashed. If had to do so,  pause this channel before changing. \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 13 : 12 & \footnotesize CHN\_PRIORITY & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Channel Request Priority. Four level of Priority  supported as list :\newline
2'b00 : Lowest priority.\newline
2'b01 : Secondary lowest priority.\newline
2'b10 : Tertiary lowest priority.\newline
2'b11 : Highest priority. \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 17 : 16 & \footnotesize AHB\_BURST\_SEL & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  AMBA Burst Select.\newline
2'b00 : INCR8;\newline
2'b01 : INCR4; \newline
2'b10 : INCR16; \newline
2'b11 : INCR16; \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 23 : 20 & \footnotesize ERR\_TYPE\_STAT\-US & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Error Configure , which would be  cleared when "ERR\_INT\_STATUS" cleared:\newline
4'b0001 : Addr Wrap Error Configure;\newline
4'b0010 : Request Mode Error Configure;\newline
4'b0100 : Length Error Configure;\newline
4'b1000 :Unalign Error Configure;\newline
1 gt;  Addr wrap error configure\newline
Set  wrap enable in standard DMA channel.\newline
2 gt;  Request mode error configure\newline
Req\_mode==2  or 3 in in standard DMA channel.\newline
3 gt;  Length Error Configure\newline
d gt;  Frag\_len==0;\newline
e gt;  Blk\_len==0;\newline
f gt;  FULL DMA channel,trsc\_len==0;\newline
4 gt;  Unalign Error Configure\newline
c gt;  Src unsigned error\newline
a1 gt;  address unalign in halfword width\newline
error=(src\_data\_size[1:0]==1) amp; amp;(src\_addr[0] =0)\newline
a2 gt;  address unalign and trsf\_step unalign in word width\newline
error=(src\_data\_size[1:0]==2) amp; amp;(src\_addr[1:0] =0)\newline
amp; amp;(src\_trsf\_step =4)\newline
d gt;  dest unsigned error\newline
b1 gt;  address unalign in halfword width\newline
error=(dst\_data\_size[1:0]==1) amp; amp;(dst\_addr[0] =0)\newline
b2 gt;  address unalign and trsf\_step unalign in word width\newline
error=(dst\_data\_size[1:0]==2) amp; amp;(dst\_addr[1:0] =0)\newline
amp; amp;(dst\_trsf\_step =4) \\
\hline                
\end{longtable}
                
\paragraph{}
\pdfbookmark[3]{14.46.4 FULL\_CHN\_INT}{anchor93}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{14.46.46 FULL\_CHN\_INT}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{14.46.4 FULL\_CHN\_INT} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x4002100C}\\
\hline
\multicolumn{5}{|l|}{\textbf{Discription} : }\\
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize CHN\_FRAGMENT\_\-INT\_EN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Fragment done interrupt enable. \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize CHN\_BLK\_INT\_\-EN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Block done interrupt enable. \\

\footnotesize 2 & \footnotesize CHN\_TRSC\_INT\_\-EN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Transaction done interrupt enable \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 3 & \footnotesize CHN\_LLIST\_INT\-\_EN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Link list done interrupt enable \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize CHN\_CFG\_ERR\_\-INT\_EN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Configuration error interrupt enable. \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 8 & \footnotesize CHN\_FRAGMENT\_\-INT\_RAW\_STATUS & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Fragment done Raw interrupt status. \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 9 & \footnotesize CHN\_BLK\_INT\_\-RAW\_STATUS & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Block done interrupt status. \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 10 & \footnotesize CHN\_TRSC\_INT\_\-RAW\_STATUS & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Channel Transaction interrupts status. \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 11 & \footnotesize CHN\_LLIST\_INT\-\_RAW\_STATUS & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Channel Link List interrupts status. \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 12 & \footnotesize CHN\_CFG\_ERR\_\-INT\_RAW\_STATUS & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Configuration error raw interrupts status. \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 16 & \footnotesize CHN\_FRAGMENT\_\-INT\_MASK\_STATUS & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Channel Fragment Masked interrupts status. \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 17 & \footnotesize CHN\_BLK\_INT\_\-MASK\_STATUS & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Channel Block Masked interrupts status. \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 18 & \footnotesize CHN\_TRSC\_INT\_\-MASK\_STATUS & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Channel Transaction Masked interrupts status. \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 19 & \footnotesize CHN\_LLIST\_INT\-\_MASK\_STATUS & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Channel Link list Masked interrupt status. \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 20 & \footnotesize CHN\_CFG\_ERR\_\-INT\_MASK\_STATUS & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Configuration error masked interrupt status. \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 24 & \footnotesize CHN\_FRAGMENT\_\-INT\_CLR & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Fragment done interrupt clear. \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 25 & \footnotesize CHN\_BLK\_INT\_\-CLR & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Block done interrupt clear. \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 26 & \footnotesize CHN\_TRSC\_INT\_\-CLR & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Transaction done interrupt clear \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 27 & \footnotesize CHN\_LLIST\_INT\-\_CLR & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Link list done interrupt clear \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 28 & \footnotesize CHN\_CFG\_ERR\_\-INT\_CLR & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Configuration error interrupt clear. \\
\hline                
\end{longtable}

\paragraph{}
\pdfbookmark[3]{14.46.7 FULL\_CHN\_FRAG\_LEN}{anchor96}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{14.46.46 FULL\_CHN\_FRAG\_LEN}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{14.46.7 FULL\_CHN\_FRAG\_LEN} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40021018}\\
\hline
\multicolumn{5}{|l|}{\textbf{Discription} : }\\
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 16 : 0 & \footnotesize FRG\_LEN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Fragment Length, with byte unit.  Max 128K-1 bytes \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 18 : 17 & \footnotesize BLK\_LEN\_REC\_\-H & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Block counter[16:15], update after each fragment  done;\newline
Note: Normally configured to 2'b0. Only to be  configured other value during state retention after wake up \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 19 & \footnotesize LLIST\_END & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Link list end flag. Active only when "LLIST\_EN"  asserted. \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 20 & \footnotesize SRC\_ADDR\_FIX\_\-EN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Source Address Fix Enable:\newline
0 : Disable Source Address Fix mode.\newline
1 : Enable Source Address Fix mode.\newline
Active High would make source address fixed within the  fragment, while "FRAG\_STEP" and "BLK\_STEP" would be applied to address after  fragment done; \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 21 & \footnotesize DEST\_ADDR\_FIX\-\_EN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Selection of the side using Address Fix mode.\newline
0 : Disable Destination Address Fix mode.\newline
1 : Enable Destination Address Fix mode.\newline
Active High would make destination address fixed within the  fragment, while "FRAG\_STEP" and "BLK\_STEP" would be applied to address after  fragment done; \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 22 & \footnotesize ADDR\_WRAP\_EN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Active High enable Address-wrapping for ring  buffer. The next address of DMA jumps to "WRAP\_START\_ADDR" address when the  current address matches "WRAP\_END\_ADDR" address:\newline
0 : Disable address wrap mode.\newline
1 : Enable address wrap mode. "WRAP\_START\_ADDR"  and "WRAP\_END\_ADDR" would be used to limit the address space of either Source  or Destination. \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 23 & \footnotesize ADDR\_WRAP\_SEL & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Selection of the side using Address wrap mode.\newline
0 : Address wrap on Source.\newline
1 : Address wrap on Destination.\newline
Only one side of a DMA channel can activate  address-wrapping mode at a time. \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 25 : 24 & \footnotesize REQ\_MODE & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Request mode, For Full DMA channel:\newline
2'b00 : Fragment;\newline
2'b01 : Block;\newline
2'b10 : Transaction;\newline
2'b11 : Link List; \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 27 : 26 & \footnotesize SWT\_MODE & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Data Switch mode selection.\newline
2'b00 : ABCD = gt; ABCD;\newline
2'b01 : ABCD = gt; DCBA\newline
2'b10 : ABCD = gt; BADC;\newline
2'b11 : ABCD = gt; CDAB \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 29 : 28 & \footnotesize DEST\_SIZE & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Destination AHB Size.\newline
2'b00 : Byte;\newline
2'b01 : Half Word\newline
2'b10 : Word\newline
2'b11 : DWord. \\
\hline                
\footnotesize 31 : 30 & \footnotesize SRC\_SIZE & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Source AHB Size.\newline
2'b00 : Byte;\newline
2'b01 : Half Word\newline
2'b10 : Word\newline
2'b11 : DWord. \\
\hline                
\end{longtable}

\hline
\multicolumn{5}{|l|}{Discription:  
          Interrupt enable register; divisor latch high when divisor latch is enable.}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize rx\_int\_en\_di\-vh\_0 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize Enable Received Data Available Interrupt; Divisor latch high bit 0 when divisor latch is enable. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize tx\_int\_en\_di\-vh\_1 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize Enable Transmit Holding Register Empty Interrupt; Divisor latch high bit 1 when divisor latch is enable. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 2 & \footnotesize lstatus\_int\_e\-n\_divh\_2 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize Enable Receiver Line Status Interrupt; Divisor latch high bit 2 when divisor latch is enable. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 & \footnotesize mstatus\_int\_e\-n\_divh\_3 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize Enable Modem Status Interrupt; Divisor latch high bit 3 when divisor latch is enable. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize divh\_4 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize Divisor latch high bit 4 when divisor latch is enable. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 5 & \footnotesize timeout\_int\_e\-n\_divh\_5 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize Enable Timeout Interrupt; Divisor latch high bit 5 when divisor latch is enable. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 6 & \footnotesize divh\_6 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize Divisor latch high bit 6 when divisor latch is enable. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 & \footnotesize ptime\_divh\_7 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize Programmable THRE Interrupt Mode Enable; Divisor latch high bit 7 when divisor latch is enable. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}

\pdfbookmark[2]{18.2 int\_en\_divh}{anchor208}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{18.2 int\_en\_divh}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{18.2 int\_en\_divh} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40033004}\\
\hline
\multicolumn{5}{|l|}{Discription:  
          Interrupt enable register; divisor latch high when divisor latch is enable.}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize rx\_int\_en\_di\-vh\_0 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize Enable Received Data Available Interrupt; Divisor latch high bit 0 when divisor latch is enable. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize tx\_int\_en\_di\-vh\_1 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize Enable Transmit Holding Register Empty Interrupt; Divisor latch high bit 1 when divisor latch is enable. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 2 & \footnotesize lstatus\_int\_e\-n\_divh\_2 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize Enable Receiver Line Status Interrupt; Divisor latch high bit 2 when divisor latch is enable. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 & \footnotesize mstatus\_int\_e\-n\_divh\_3 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize Enable Modem Status Interrupt; Divisor latch high bit 3 when divisor latch is enable. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize divh\_4 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize Divisor latch high bit 4 when divisor latch is enable. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 5 & \footnotesize timeout\_int\_e\-n\_divh\_5 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize Enable Timeout Interrupt; Divisor latch high bit 5 when divisor latch is enable. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 6 & \footnotesize divh\_6 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize Divisor latch high bit 6 when divisor latch is enable. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 & \footnotesize ptime\_divh\_7 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize Programmable THRE Interrupt Mode Enable; Divisor latch high bit 7 when divisor latch is enable. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}

\multicolumn{4}{|l}{19.2 int\_en\_divh} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40034004}\\
\hline
\multicolumn{5}{|l|}{Discription:  
          Interrupt enable register; divisor latch high when divisor latch is enable.}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize rx\_int\_en\_di\-vh\_0 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize Enable Received Data Available Interrupt; Divisor latch high bit 0 when divisor latch is enable. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize tx\_int\_en\_di\-vh\_1 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize Enable Transmit Holding Register Empty Interrupt; Divisor latch high bit 1 when divisor latch is enable. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 2 & \footnotesize lstatus\_int\_e\-n\_divh\_2 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize Enable Receiver Line Status Interrupt; Divisor latch high bit 2 when divisor latch is enable. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 & \footnotesize mstatus\_int\_e\-n\_divh\_3 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize Enable Modem Status Interrupt; Divisor latch high bit 3 when divisor latch is enable. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize divh\_4 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize Divisor latch high bit 4 when divisor latch is enable. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 5 & \footnotesize timeout\_int\_e\-n\_divh\_5 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize Enable Timeout Interrupt; Divisor latch high bit 5 when divisor latch is enable. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 6 & \footnotesize divh\_6 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize Divisor latch high bit 6 when divisor latch is enable. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 & \footnotesize ptime\_divh\_7 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize Programmable THRE Interrupt Mode Enable; Divisor latch high bit 7 when divisor latch is enable. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}

\section{sd\_host}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{21.1 ctrl}{anchor301}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{21.1 ctrl}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{21.1 ctrl} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40036000}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 2 : 0 & \footnotesize endian & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize Endian Type\newline
SD TX FIFO Endian\newline
3'b000: \{pwdata\_byte[3],pwdata\_byte[2],pwdata\_byte[1],pwdata\_byte[0]\}\newline
3'b001: \{pwdata\_byte[0],pwdata\_byte[1],pwdata\_byte[2],pwdata\_byte[3]\}\newline
3'b010: \{pwdata\_byte[1],pwdata\_byte[0],pwdata\_byte[3],pwdata\_byte[2]\}\newline
3'b011: pwdata\_bit[0-31]\newline
3'b100: \{pwdata\_byte[2],pwdata\_byte[3],pwdata\_byte[0],pwdata\_byte[1]\}\newline
default: 32'h0 \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 & \footnotesize reset\_n & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize Software reset, active Low\newline
Software reset for TX and RX FIFOs of Sd host, active low \\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize dma\_rx\_en & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize Enable rx dma req triggered when rxfifo level arrived dma\_rx\_th \\
\hline
\footnotesize 5 & \footnotesize dma\_tx\_en & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize Enable tx dma req triggered when txfifo level arrived dma\_tx\_th \\
\hline
\footnotesize 11 : 8 & \footnotesize dma\_rx\_th & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize Rx dma req triggered threshold \\
\hline
\footnotesize 19 : 16 & \footnotesize dma\_tx\_th & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize Tx dma req triggered threshold \\
\hline
\end{longtable}

\hline
\end{longtable}

\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\

\hline

1'b1: flush status\newline
1'b0: normal status \\

\pdfbookmark[2]{22.7 aud\_intf\_i2s\_cfg0}{anchor328}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{22.7 aud\_intf\_i2s\_cfg0}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{22.7 aud\_intf\_i2s\_cfg0} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x4003701C}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 : 0 & \footnotesize swap\_chlr\_0 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Swap Channel L and Channel R.[0]\newline
[0] for output channel swap, 0: left, 1:right\newline
[1] for input channel swap, 0: left, 1: right \\
\hline
\footnotesize 2 & \footnotesize tx\_data\_half\_\-cycle\_dly\_0 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  delayed Audio data out by half cycle.\newline
0:: NO\_DLY\newline
1:: DLY \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 & \footnotesize lsb\_0 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  When high, the output data format is with the least significant bit first.\newline
0:: MSB\newline
1:: LSB \\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize right\_justifie\-d\_0 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  right justified mode. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 9 : 5 & \footnotesize bck\_lrck\_0 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  configure extend cycles of bck after data transfer is finished.\newline
Voice\_Mode:: each sample takes wlen + tx\_bck\_lrck BCLK cycle. \newline
Audio\_Mode:: each sample takes 2*(wlen + tx\_bck\_lrck) BCLK cycle. 2 times than Voice \\
\hline
\footnotesize 11 : 10 & \footnotesize wlen\_0 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  i2s data length\newline
00: 16-bit\newline
01: 20-bit\newline
10: 24-bit\newline
11: 32-bit \\
\hline
\footnotesize 12 & \footnotesize lrck\_pol\_0 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  configure LRCK polarity. \newline
0:: LEFT\_H\_RIGHT\_L:    high level on LRCK means left channel, low level on LRCK means right channel. \newline
1:: LEFT\_L\_RIGHT\_H:    high level on LRCK means right channel, low level on LRCK means left channel. \newline
Note: this bit should be set to '0' (LEFT\_H\_RIGHT\_L) in voice mode. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 13 & \footnotesize bck\_pol\_0 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  if Master Mode, invert BCLK out. if slave Mode, invert BCLK in.\newline
0:: NORMAL\newline
1:: INVERT \\
\hline
\footnotesize 14 & \footnotesize bckout\_gate\_0 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Sets the BckOut gating. This bit decide if aud intf continue to output BCK clock after data has been sent.\newline
0:: NO\_GATE\newline
1:: GATED \\
\hline
\footnotesize 15 & \footnotesize rx\_half\_cycle\-\_dly\_0 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  delayed Audio input data by half cycle.\newline
0:: NO\_DLY\newline
1:: DLY \\
\hline
\footnotesize 16 & \footnotesize tx\_lrck\_half\_\-cycle\_dly\_0 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  delayed Audio LRCK by half cycle.\newline
0:: NO\_DLY\newline
1:: DLY \\
\hline
\footnotesize 18 : 17 & \footnotesize rx\_dly\_0 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Indicates the delay between serial data in MSB and LRCK edge. \newline
2'b00:: ALIGN:    Digital audio in MSB is aligned with LRCLK edge. \newline
2'b01:: DLY\_1:   Digital audio in MSB is 1 cycle delayed to LRCLK edge. \newline
2'b10:: DLY\_2:   Digital audio in MSB is 2 cycle delayed to LRCLK edge. \newline
2'b11:: DLY\_3:   Digital audio in MSB is 3 cycle delayed to LRCLK edge. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 19 & \footnotesize tx\_dly\_s\_0 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  ONLY for slave mode:: configure 1 cycle supplementary Tx delay. \newline
0:: NO\_DLY:  No supplementary Tx delay. \newline
1:: DLY:         One Cycle supplementary Tx delay. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 20 & \footnotesize tx\_dly\_0 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  configure the delay between serial data out MSB and LRCK edge. \newline
0:: ALIGN:           Digital audio out MSB is aligned with LRCLK edge. \newline
1:: DLY\_1:          Digital audio out MSB is 1 cycle delayed to LRCLK edge \\
\hline
\footnotesize 21 & \footnotesize master\_mode\_0 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  configure aud intf works in master mode (LRCLK and BCK timing signals are generated internally) or slave mode (LRCLK and BCK timing signals are generated externally).\newline
0:: SLAVE\newline
1:: MASTER \\
\hline
\footnotesize 24 : 22 & \footnotesize rx\_serial\_mod\-e\_0 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Configure serial aud intf mode. \newline
0:: I2S\_PCM\newline
1:: VOICE\newline
2:: DAI\newline
When mode is set DAI, the bit Master Mode should be set to '1'. Data should be sent out on falling edge, which requires either Bclk\_Pol = '0' and Half\_Cycle\_DLY = '1' or Bclk\_Pol = '1' and Half\_Cycle\_DLY = '0'. Bits Tx\_DLY and BCKOut\_Gate must be configured to '0' and '1'. \newline
The DAI mode must NOT be modified after aud intf is enabled. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 27 : 25 & \footnotesize tx\_serial\_mod\-e\_0 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Configure serial aud intf mode. \newline
0:: I2S\_PCM\newline
1:: VOICE\newline
2:: DAI\newline
When mode is set DAI, the bit Master Mode should be set to '1'. Data should be sent out on falling edge, which requires either Bclk\_Pol = '0' and Half\_Cycle\_DLY = '1' or Bclk\_Pol = '1' and Half\_Cycle\_DLY = '0'. Bits Tx\_DLY and BCKOut\_Gate must be configured to '0' and '1'. \newline
The DAI mode must NOT be modified after aud intf is enabled. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 28 & \footnotesize tx\_off\_0 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  Disable aud intf Tx functions. Important:: if you want to do record only, you must set this bit otherwise aud intf state machine will not start. \newline
0:: TX\_ON:    Both Tx Rx enabled. \newline
1:: TX\_OFF:   Rx enabled only, Tx disabled. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 30 : 29 & \footnotesize tx\_mode\_0 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Configure mono or stereo format for Audio data out:\newline
2'b00:: STEREO\_STEREO:  stereo input from DMA, stereo output to pin. \newline
2'b01:: MONOL\_STEREO\_DUPLI:   mono channel L input from DMA, stereo output duplicate in both channels to pin. \newline
2'b10:: MONOR\_STEREO\_DUPLI:  mono channel R input from DMA, stereo output duplicate in both channels to pin. \newline
2'b11:: MONO\_IN\_MONO\_OUT\newline
Funtion mono input from DMA, stereo output in left channel to pin, can be implemented by setting aud intf\_tx\_cfg\_ch[1] or aud intf\_tx\_cfg\_ch[0] disable.\newline
When works in DAI, or DSD mode, always select "00" mode STEREO\_STEREO. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 & \footnotesize i2s\_enable\_0 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  enable i2s interface \\
\hline
\end{longtable}

\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{22.8 aud\_intf\_i2s\_cfg1}

0:: LEFT\_H\_RIGHT\_L:    high level on LRCK means left channel, low level on LRCK means right channel. \newline
1:: LEFT\_L\_RIGHT\_H:    high level on LRCK means right channel, low level on LRCK means left channel. \newline
Note: this bit should be set to '0' (LEFT\_H\_RIGHT\_L) in voice mode. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 13 & \footnotesize bck\_pol\_1 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  if Master Mode, invert BCLK out. if slave Mode, invert BCLK in.\newline
0:: NORMAL\newline
1:: INVERT \\
\hline
\footnotesize 14 & \footnotesize bckout\_gate\_1 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Sets the BckOut gating. This bit decide if aud intf continue to output BCK clock after data has been sent.\newline
0:: NO\_GATE\newline
1:: GATED \\
\hline
\footnotesize 15 & \footnotesize rx\_half\_cycle\-\_dly\_1 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  delayed Audio input data by half cycle.\newline
0:: NO\_DLY\newline
1:: DLY \\
\hline
\footnotesize 16 & \footnotesize tx\_lrck\_half\_\-cycle\_dly\_1 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  delayed Audio output LRCK by half cycle.\newline
0:: NO\_DLY\newline
1:: DLY \\
\hline
\footnotesize 18 : 17 & \footnotesize rx\_dly\_1 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Indicates the delay between serial data in MSB and LRCK edge. \newline
2'b00:: ALIGN:    Digital audio in MSB is aligned with LRCLK edge. \newline
2'b01:: DLY\_1:   Digital audio in MSB is 1 cycle delayed to LRCLK edge. \newline
2'b10:: DLY\_2:   Digital audio in MSB is 2 cycle delayed to LRCLK edge. \newline
2'b11:: DLY\_3:   Digital audio in MSB is 3 cycle delayed to LRCLK edge. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 19 & \footnotesize tx\_dly\_s\_1 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  ONLY for slave mode:: configure 1 cycle supplementary Tx delay. \newline
0:: NO\_DLY:  No supplementary Tx delay. \newline
1:: DLY:         One Cycle supplementary Tx delay. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 20 & \footnotesize tx\_dly\_1 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  configure the delay between serial data out MSB and LRCK edge. \newline
0:: ALIGN:           Digital audio out MSB is aligned with LRCLK edge. \newline
1:: DLY\_1:          Digital audio out MSB is 1 cycle delayed to LRCLK edge \\
\hline
\footnotesize 21 & \footnotesize master\_mode\_1 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  configure aud intf works in master mode (LRCLK and BCK timing signals are generated internally) or slave mode (LRCLK and BCK timing signals are generated externally).\newline
0:: SLAVE\newline
1:: MASTER \\
\hline
\footnotesize 24 : 22 & \footnotesize rx\_serial\_mod\-e\_1 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Configure serial aud intf mode. \newline
0:: I2S\_PCM\newline
1:: VOICE\newline
2:: DAI\newline
When mode is set DAI, the bit Master Mode should be set to '1'. Data should be sent out on falling edge, which requires either Bclk\_Pol = '0' and Half\_Cycle\_DLY = '1' or Bclk\_Pol = '1' and Half\_Cycle\_DLY = '0'. Bits Tx\_DLY and BCKOut\_Gate must be configured to '0' and '1'. \newline
The DAI mode must NOT be modified after aud intf is enabled. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 27 : 25 & \footnotesize tx\_serial\_mod\-e\_1 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Configure serial aud intf mode. \newline
0:: I2S\_PCM\newline
1:: VOICE\newline
2:: DAI\newline
When mode is set DAI, the bit Master Mode should be set to '1'. Data should be sent out on falling edge, which requires either Bclk\_Pol = '0' and Half\_Cycle\_DLY = '1' or Bclk\_Pol = '1' and Half\_Cycle\_DLY = '0'. Bits Tx\_DLY and BCKOut\_Gate must be configured to '0' and '1'. \newline
The DAI mode must NOT be modified after aud intf is enabled. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 28 & \footnotesize tx\_off\_1 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  Disable aud intf Tx functions. Important:: if you want to do record only, you must set this bit otherwise aud intf state machine will not start. \newline
0:: TX\_ON:    Both Tx Rx enabled. \newline
1:: TX\_OFF:   Rx enabled only, Tx disabled. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 30 : 29 & \footnotesize tx\_mode\_1 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Configure mono or stereo format for Audio data out:\newline
2'b00:: STEREO\_STEREO:             stereo input from DMA, stereo output to pin. \newline
2'b01:: MONOL\_STEREO\_DUPLI:   mono channel L input from DMA, stereo output duplicate in both channels to pin. \newline
2'b10:: MONOR\_STEREO\_DUPLI:  mono channel R input from DMA, stereo output duplicate in both channels to pin. \newline
2'b11:: MONO\_IN\_MONO\_OUT\newline
Funtion mono input from DMA, stereo output in left channel to pin, can be implemented by setting aud intf\_tx\_cfg\_ch[1] or aud intf\_tx\_cfg\_ch[0] disable.\newline
When works in DAI, or DSD mode, always select "00" mode STEREO\_STEREO. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 & \footnotesize i2s\_enable\_1 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  enable i2s interface \\
\hline
\end{longtable}

1:: LOOPBACK \\
\hline
\footnotesize 14 & \footnotesize bckout\_auto\_g\-ate\_bypass\_0 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  1: bypss bck auto  gate, keep bck output always ok \\
\hline
\footnotesize 18 : 17 & \footnotesize rx\_mode\_0 & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  \\
\hline
\footnotesize 19 & \footnotesize i2s\_chn\_out\_\-sel & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  1:i2s tx sel channel 1; 0:i2s tx sel channel 0 \\
\hline
\footnotesize 20 & \footnotesize i2s\_chn\_in\_s\-el & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  1:i2s rx sel channel 1; 0:i2s rx sel channel 0 \\

\headinginfo{wdg0}
\section{wdg0}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{27.1 WDG\_LOAD\_VAL}{anchor481}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{27.1 WDG\_LOAD\_VAL}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{27.1 WDG\_LOAD\_VAL} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40103000}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 0 & \footnotesize wdg\_load\_val & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x3FFFF & \footnotesize  watchdog  timer load value.\newline
In reset mode, software should  load new value before timer decrease to 0.\newline
In interrupt mode, this value  is counting start number.\newline
The default value is about 8  seconds. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{27.2 WDG\_CNT}{anchor482}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{27.2 WDG\_CNT}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{27.2 WDG\_CNT} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40103004}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 0 & \footnotesize wdg\_cnt & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0xFFFFFFFF & \footnotesize  watchdog counter value \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{27.3 WDG\_CTRL}{anchor483}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{27.3 WDG\_CTRL}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{27.3 WDG\_CTRL} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40103008}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize wdg\_irq\_en & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Watchdog interrupt enable bit\newline
0: interrupt is disabled\newline
1: interrupt is enabled\newline
For reset mode: wdg\_rst\_en =1,  wdg\_irq\_en=0.\newline
For interrupt mode: wdg\_rst\_en  =0, wdg\_irq\_en=1.\newline
For combined mode: wdg\_rst\_en  =1, wdg\_irq\_en=1. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize wdg\_run & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Watchdog counter open:\newline
0: counter stops.\newline
1: counter runs. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{27.4 WDG\_INT\_CLR}{anchor484}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{27.4 WDG\_INT\_CLR}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{27.4 WDG\_INT\_CLR} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x4010300C}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize wdg\_int\_clr & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Watchdog interrupt clear\newline
Write 1 to this bit to clear  interrupt\newline
Read this bit always get 0. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{27.5 WDG\_INT\_RAW}{anchor485}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{27.5 WDG\_INT\_RAW}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{27.5 WDG\_INT\_RAW} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40103010}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize wdg\_int\_raw & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Watchdog interrupt raw status. Watch  dog reset cannot clear this raw status. Write wdg\_int\_clr can clear this raw  status. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize wdg\_ld\_busy & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Watchdog  load busy status\newline
0:  Watchdog is ready for new loading\newline
1: Last  loading is not completed\newline
Software  must not load new value when this bit is busy, that is, this bit should be  checked before any new loading.\newline
This  bit is set after a new loading, and lasts two or three RTC clock cycles,  about 60us - 92us. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{27.6 WDG\_INT\_MASK}{anchor486}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{27.6 WDG\_INT\_MASK}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{27.6 WDG\_INT\_MASK} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40103014}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize wdg\_int\_mask & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Watchdog interrupt masked  status \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{27.7 WDG\_LOCK}{anchor487}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{27.7 WDG\_LOCK}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{27.7 WDG\_LOCK} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40103C00}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 15 : 0 & \footnotesize wdg\_lock & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Watchdog lock control\newline
Write 32'h1ACCE551 to this register to unlock  watchdog.\newline
Write other value to this  register to lock watchdog\newline
If reading this register, bit-0  is lock status, and other bits are reserved.\newline
If watchdog is locked, all  control registers cannot be written by software. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}

\pdfbookmark[2]{29.1 SPI\_TXD}{anchor495}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{29.1 SPI\_TXD}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{29.1 SPI\_TXD} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40105000}\\
\hline
\multicolumn{5}{|l|}{Discription: Write data to tx fifo
Read data from rx fifo}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 0 & \footnotesize SPI\_TXD\_RXD & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Write data to this address initiates a  character transmission through TX FIFO\newline
Read from this address retrieve data from  RX FIFO \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{29.2 SPI\_CLKD}{anchor496}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{29.2 SPI\_CLKD}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{29.2 SPI\_CLKD} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40105004}\\
\hline
\multicolumn{5}{|l|}{Discription: Clock divider bit 15 to 0.divider is 2(n+1)}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 15 : 0 & \footnotesize SPI\_CLKD & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x3 & \footnotesize Specify the clock ratio between spi\_sck and clk\_spi\newline
spi\_sck = clk\_spi/2(SPI\_CLKD + 1) \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{29.3 SPI\_CTL0}{anchor497}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{29.3 SPI\_CTL0}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{29.3 SPI\_CTL0} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40105008}\\
\hline
\multicolumn{5}{|l|}{Discription: SPI control register}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize NG\_RX & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  "1" enable RX data shift in at clock neg-edge \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize NG\_TX & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  "1" enable TX data shift out at clock neg-edge \\
\hline
\footnotesize 6 : 2 & \footnotesize CHNL\_LEN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  "0" 32 bits per word\newline
"1" 1 bits per word \newline
"31" 31 bits per word \\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 & \footnotesize LSB & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  "1": enable transmit data from LSB \\
\hline
\footnotesize 11 : 8 & \footnotesize SPI\_CSN\_PRE & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0xF & \footnotesize  "1": cs is invalid \newline
"0": cs is valid \\
\hline
\footnotesize 12 & \footnotesize Reserved & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Reserved \\
\hline
\footnotesize 13 & \footnotesize SCK\_REV & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  SCK inverse enable \\
\hline
\footnotesize 14 & \footnotesize SYNC\_MD & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  "1" : sync mode \\
\hline
\footnotesize 15 & \footnotesize SYNC\_POL & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Sync polarity, positive or negative pulse for SPI or 3-wire mode, read command polarity \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{29.4 SPI\_CTL1}{anchor498}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{29.4 SPI\_CTL1}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{29.4 SPI\_CTL1} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x4010500C}\\
\hline
\multicolumn{5}{|l|}{Discription: SPI control register}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 : 0 & \footnotesize S3W\_POS & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  3-wire mode, w/r control position or the sync pulse position \\
\hline
\footnotesize 5 & \footnotesize S3W\_MD & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  "1" enable 3-wire mode \\
\hline
\footnotesize 6 & \footnotesize CS\_H\_MD & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  3-wire melody timing 1, csn high mode enable \\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 & \footnotesize S8\_MD & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  enable S8 mode \\
\hline
\footnotesize 11 : 8 & \footnotesize SYN\_CSN\_SEL & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  S8 CD or SYNC signal maps to csn number\newline
0x1 selects csn0 as cd signal\newline
0x2 selects csn1 as cd signal\newline
in SPI\_HS it must be 0x0 \\
\hline
\footnotesize 13 : 12 & \footnotesize RTX\_MD & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  "0x00":idle mode\newline
"0x01":receive mode\newline
"0x10":transmit mode\newline
"0x11":transmit and receive \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{29.5 SPI\_CTL2}{anchor499}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{29.5 SPI\_CTL2}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{29.5 SPI\_CTL2} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40105010}\\
\hline
\multicolumn{5}{|l|}{Discription: SPI control register}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 : 0 & \footnotesize S3W\_RD\_START & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Read date start bit, used for 3-wire mode and 3-wire 9bit RW mode \\
\hline
\footnotesize 5 & \footnotesize IS\_SLVD & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  "1" slave,only support microplus mode\newline
"0" master \\
\hline
\footnotesize 6 & \footnotesize DMA\_EN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  "1" : enable DMA \\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 & \footnotesize RX\_ONLY\_HLD & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  "0" : working on only receive mode, when rxf\_realfull is high, SPI will be held until rxf realfull is low \\
\hline
\footnotesize 8 & \footnotesize RX\_DMA\_SEL & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  "0" : rx\_dma\_req keep high until receiving the rx\_dma\_ack\newline
"1" : rx\_dma\_req is high when rx full is high \\
\hline
\footnotesize 9 & \footnotesize TX\_DMA\_SEL & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  "0" : tx\_dma\_req keep high until receiving the tx\_dma\_ack\newline
"1" : tx\_dma\_req is high when tx full is high \\
\hline
\footnotesize 10 & \footnotesize DMA\_REQ\_SEL & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  "1" enable 3-wire mode \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{29.6 SPI\_CTL3}{anchor500}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{29.6 SPI\_CTL3}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{29.6 SPI\_CTL3} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40105014}\\
\hline
\multicolumn{5}{|l|}{Discription: SPI control register}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 : 0 & \footnotesize RXF\_FULL\_THLD & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x10 & \footnotesize  Receive data empty threshold \\
\hline
\footnotesize 12 : 8 & \footnotesize RXF\_EMPTY\_THL\-D & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x10 & \footnotesize  Receive data full threshold \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{29.7 SPI\_CTL4}{anchor501}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{29.7 SPI\_CTL4}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{29.7 SPI\_CTL4} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40105018}\\
\hline
\multicolumn{5}{|l|}{Discription: SPI control register}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 8 : 0 & \footnotesize BLOCK\_NUM & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Number of data words ready to receive in "receive only" mode.\newline
Only used for master mode \\
\hline
\footnotesize 9 & \footnotesize IS\_RX\_ONLY & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  "1":receive data only.The bits should be written at last.\newline
Only used for master mode \\
\hline
\footnotesize 10 & \footnotesize SYNC\_HALF & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Sync half,sync width is half spi\_sck cycle \\
\hline
\footnotesize 11 & \footnotesize SYNC\_CLKMASK & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  "1" Mask out the first clock pulse in SPI mode \\
\hline
\footnotesize 13 : 12 & \footnotesize PHS\_DLY & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Phase delay. Relate to fast mode\newline
When in normal mode,this bit is not used. Only used for slave mode \\
\hline
\footnotesize 14 & \footnotesize IS\_FST & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  "0": normal mode\newline
"1": fast mode \\
\hline
\footnotesize 15 & \footnotesize RX\_ONLY\_DO & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  "0": SPI send all 0 to slave\newline
"1": SPI send all 1 to slave \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{29.8 SPI\_CTL5}{anchor502}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{29.8 SPI\_CTL5}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{29.8 SPI\_CTL5} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x4010501C}\\
\hline
\multicolumn{5}{|l|}{Discription: SPI control register}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 15 : 0 & \footnotesize ITVL\_NUM & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize For master, transmit data interval, programmabl n from 0 to 65535, delay is (4*n+3) clock cycle\newline
For slave, max receive data interval. If slave has not sampled data,in the interval (4*n+3) spi\_clk,slave will stop the receive process and send timeout interrupt \\
\hline
\end{longtable}

\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{29.9 SPI\_INT\_EN}

\hline

\hline
\footnotesize 5 & \footnotesize TIME\_OUT\_INT\_\-EN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Slave mode timeout interrupt enable \\
\hline
\footnotesize 6 & \footnotesize RXF\_FULL\_INT\_\-EN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Rx\_fifo\_full interrupt enable \\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 & \footnotesize TXF\_EMPTY\_INT\-\_EN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Tx\_fifo\_empty interrupt enable \\
\hline
\footnotesize 8 & \footnotesize TXF\_END\_INT\_\-EN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Tx end interrupt enable \\
\hline
\footnotesize 9 & \footnotesize RXF\_END\_INT\_\-EN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Rx end interrupt enable \\

\hline

\hline

\hline
\footnotesize 3 & \footnotesize TXF\_EMPTY\_INT\-\_CLR & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Write "1" clear Tx\_fifo\_empty interrupt \\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize RX\_OVF\_INT\_C\-LR & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Write "1" clear Rx\_overrun\_reg interrupt \\
\hline
\footnotesize 5 & \footnotesize TIME\_OUT\_INT\_\-CLR & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Write "1" clear slave mode timeout interrupt \\
\hline
\footnotesize 8 & \footnotesize TXF\_END\_INT\_\-CLR & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Tx data end interrupt clear \\
\hline
\footnotesize 9 & \footnotesize RXF\_END\_INT\_\-CLR & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Rx data end interrupt clear \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{29.11 SPI\_INT\_RAW\_STS}{anchor505}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{29.11 SPI\_INT\_RAW\_STS}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{29.11 SPI\_INT\_RAW\_STS} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40105028}\\
\hline
\multicolumn{5}{|l|}{Discription: SPI interrupt raw status}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize RX\_FIFO\_FULL\_\-R & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Rx\_fifo\_full\_r \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize RXF\_EMPTY\_RAW\-\_STS & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  Raw rx\_fifo\_empty interrupt \\
\hline
\footnotesize 2 & \footnotesize TXF\_FULL\_RAW\_\-STS & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Raw Tx\_fifo\_full interrupt \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 & \footnotesize TX\_FIFO\_EMPTY\-\_W & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  Raw txf\_fifo\_empty\_empty interrupt \\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize RX\_OVF\_RAW\_S\-TS & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Raw Rx\_overrun\_reg interrupt \\
\hline

\hline
\footnotesize 6 & \footnotesize RXF\_FULL\_RAW\_\-STS & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Raw rxf\_full interrupt. when the number of rx fifo data byte is larger than the the rx empty watermark value.Atuo clear \\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 & \footnotesize TXF\_EMPTY\_RAW\-\_STS & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  Raw txf\_empty interrupt. when the number of tx fifo data byte is less than the the tx empty watermark value.Atuo clear \\
\hline
\footnotesize 8 & \footnotesize TX\_END\_IRQ & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Raw tx data end interrupt. bit is set when spi controller send TX\_DATA\_LEN data \\
\hline
\footnotesize 9 & \footnotesize RX\_END\_IRQ & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Raw rx data end interrupt. bit is set when spi controller receive RX\_DATA\_LEN data \\
\hline
\end{longtable}

\pdfbookmark[2]{29.13 SPI\_STS1}{anchor507}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{29.13 SPI\_STS1}

\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{29.13 SPI\_STS1} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40105030}\\
\hline
\multicolumn{5}{|l|}{Discription: SPI status register}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 : 0 & \footnotesize RXF\_RD\_ADDR & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  RX FIFO read address \\
\hline
\footnotesize 12 : 8 & \footnotesize RXF\_WR\_ADDR & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  RX FIFO write address \\

\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{29.14 SPI\_STS2}{anchor508}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{29.14 SPI\_STS2}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{29.14 SPI\_STS2} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40105034}\\
\hline
\multicolumn{5}{|l|}{Discription: SPI status register}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize RXF\_FULL & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  This bit is set when the number of RX FIFO data byte is larger than the RX full watermark value. Auto cleared when the condition disappears. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize RXF\_EMPTY & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  This bit is set when the number of RX FIFO data byte is less than the RX empty interrupt watermark value. Auto cleared when the condition disappears. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 2 & \footnotesize TXF\_FULL & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  This bit is set when the number of TX FIFO data byte is larger than the TX full watermark value. Auto cleared when the condition disappears. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 & \footnotesize TXF\_EMPTY & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  This bit is set when the number of TX FIFO  data byte is less than the TX empty watermark value. Auto cleared when the condition disappears. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize RXF\_REALFULL & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  RX FIFO is real full.(not relates to register RXF\_FULL\_THLD) \\
\hline
\footnotesize 5 & \footnotesize RXF\_REALEMPTY & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  RX FIFO is real empty. RX FIFO has no data \\
\hline
\footnotesize 6 & \footnotesize TXF\_REALFULL & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  TX FIFO is real full.(not relates to register TXF\_FULL\_THLD) \\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 & \footnotesize TXF\_REALEMPTY & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  TX FIFO is real empty. TX FIFO has no data \\
\hline
\footnotesize 8 & \footnotesize BUSY & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  "1": busy, transmit process\newline
"0": idle state \\
\hline
\footnotesize 9 & \footnotesize SPI\_RXD & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Spi\_rxd(monitor) \\
\hline
\footnotesize 10 & \footnotesize SPI\_TXD & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Spi\_txd(monitor) \\
\hline
\footnotesize 11 & \footnotesize SPI\_SCK & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Spi\_sck(monitor) \\
\hline
\footnotesize 12 & \footnotesize SPI\_CS & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Spi\_cs(monitor) \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{29.15 DSP\_WAIT}{anchor509}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{29.15 DSP\_WAIT}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{29.15 DSP\_WAIT} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40105038}\\
\hline
\multicolumn{5}{|l|}{Discription: Use for DSP control}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 : 0 & \footnotesize SPI\_DSPWAIT & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  This register is used for DSP control \\
\hline
\footnotesize 5 : 4 & \footnotesize RX\_DATA\_SWT & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Read data switch:\newline
0: rdata[31:0] = pdata[31:0]\newline
1: rdata = \{pdata[7:0], pdata[15:8], pdata[23:16],  pdata[31:24]\}\newline
2: rdata = \{pdata[15:0],pdata[31:16]\} \\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 : 6 & \footnotesize TX\_DATA\_SWT & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Write data switch:\newline
0: wdata[31:0] = pdata[31:0]\newline
1: wdata = \{pdata[7:0], pdata[15:8], pdata[23:16],  pdata[31:24]\}\newline
2: wdata = \{pdata[15:0],pdata[31:16]\} \\
\hline
\end{longtable}

\pdfbookmark[2]{29.20 SPI\_CTL7}{anchor514}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{29.20 SPI\_CTL7}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{29.20 SPI\_CTL7} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x4010504C}\\
\hline
\multicolumn{5}{|l|}{Discription: SPI control register}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize CSN\_IE\_CTL & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  CSN IE output set\newline
0: not support csn input\newline
1: support csn input \\
\hline
\footnotesize 2 : 1 & \footnotesize CSN\_I\_SEL & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  CSN select control:\newline
0: CSN 0    1: CSN 1    2: CSN 2    3: CSN 3 \\
\hline
\footnotesize 5 : 3 & \footnotesize SPI\_MODE & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Used for master only\newline
0: SPI\_MODE disable\newline
1: 3 wire 9 bit, cd bit, SDI/SDO share one IO\newline
2: 3 wire 9 bit, cd bit, SDI,SDO\newline
3: 4 wire 8 bit, cd pin, SDI/SDO\newline
4: 4 wire 8 bit, cd pin, SDI,SDO \\
\hline
\footnotesize 6 & \footnotesize TX\_CMD\_SET & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  1: select fmark as the dma request\newline
0:select software dma request \\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 & \footnotesize SPI\_TX\_HLD\_E\-N & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  1: enable ahb2apb bridge write hold when tx fifo full\newline
0: disable ahb2apb bridge write hold \\
\hline
\footnotesize 8 & \footnotesize SPI\_RX\_HLD\_E\-N & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  1: enable ahb2apb bridge read hold when rx fifo empty\newline
0: disable ahb2apb bridge write hold \\
\hline
\footnotesize 9 & \footnotesize SPI\_SLV\_CLK\_\-SEl & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  \\
\hline
\footnotesize 10 & \footnotesize SPI\_SLV\_EN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Used when SPI slave in High speed mode\newline
1:enable spi slave rtx\newline
0:disable spi slave rtx \\
\hline
\footnotesize 11 & \footnotesize SPI\_SLV\_SEL & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  1: SPI slave in low speed mode\newline
0: SPI slave in low speed mode \\
\hline
\footnotesize 12 & \footnotesize RGB\_888\_EN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  \\
\hline
\footnotesize 13 & \footnotesize RGB\_666\_EN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  \\
\hline
\footnotesize 14 & \footnotesize RGB\_565\_EN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  \\
\hline
\footnotesize 15 & \footnotesize DATA\_LINE2\_EN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  \\
\hline
\end{longtable}

\pdfbookmark[2]{35.4 GPIOIS}{anchor568}

\hline
\footnotesize 19 : 0 & \footnotesize GPIOIS & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0xFFFFF & \footnotesize  "1" detect signals level\newline
"0" detect signals edge \\

\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{35.5 GPIOIBE}{anchor569}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{35.5 GPIOIBE}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}

"0" interrupt generation event is controlled by  GPIOIEV \\

\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{35.6 GPIOIEV}{anchor570}

"1" high levels trigger interrupts,\newline
"0" low levels trigger interrupts. \\

\end{longtable}

\hline

"1" corresponding bit interrupt is enabled.\newline
"0" corresponding bit interrupt isn't enabled \\

\end{longtable}

\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 16 & \footnotesize EFUSE\_ALL0\_ST\-ART\_INDEX & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize All 0 check start index \\

\pdfbookmark[2]{36.2 EFUSE\_MODE\_CTRL}{anchor580}

\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize EFUSE\_ALL0\_CH\-ECK\_START & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize write 1 to this bit will trigger all0 check to efuse, this bit is self-clear\newline
,read this bit will always get 0.(use PREADY) \\
