\pdfbookmark[2]{36.3 EFUSE\_IP\_VER\_reg}{anchor581}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{36.3 EFUSE\_IP\_VER\_reg}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{36.3 EFUSE\_IP\_VER\_reg} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x4010C014}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 15 : 0 & \footnotesize EFUSE\_IP\_VER & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x100 & \footnotesize IP version, now is r1p0 \\
\hline
\footnotesize 17 : 16 & \footnotesize EFUSE\_TYPE & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize Efuse type: 00 TSMC \\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 18 & \footnotesize reserved\_0 & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize RESERVED \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{36.4 EFUSE\_CFG0}{anchor582}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{36.4 EFUSE\_CFG0}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{36.4 EFUSE\_CFG0} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x4010C018}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 8 : 0 & \footnotesize TPGM\_TIME\_CNT & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x140 & \footnotesize Program strobe high time. If set n, the Tpgm time will last for (n+1) clk\_efuse cycle, only when PGM\_EN=1 can write this field. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 15 : 9 & \footnotesize reserved\_0 & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize RESERVED \\
\hline
\footnotesize 23 : 16 & \footnotesize EFUSE\_STROBE\_\-LOW\_WIDTH & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x1C & \footnotesize This counter is used to control STROBE signal low level width in PGM mode, for TSMC efuse memory, no extra requirement for this signal, For 26Mhz efuse controller clock, by default, this width will be: 38.4*28=1075ns > 1us.\newline
If you want to speed up program speed, can configure the register to a smaller value. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 24 & \footnotesize CLK\_EFS\_DIV & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize Clk\_efs divider, if this value is n, the frequency of controller will be divided by (n+1) from clk\_efs.\newline
In most case, this field not need to change. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{36.5 EFUSE\_SEC\_EN}{anchor583}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{36.5 EFUSE\_SEC\_EN}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{36.5 EFUSE\_SEC\_EN} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x4010C040}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize SEC\_VDD\_EN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize efuse vdd enable \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize SEC\_AUTO\_CHEC\-K\_ENABLE & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize program read back auto-check enable \\
\hline
\footnotesize 2 & \footnotesize DOUBLE\_BIT\_EN\-\_SEC & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize efuse double bit enable \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 & \footnotesize SEC\_MARGIN\_RD\-\_ENABLE & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize efuse margin read mode enable \\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize SEC\_LOCK\_BIT\_\-WR\_EN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize If set the bit, lock bits will be written after PGM process. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 5 & \footnotesize reserved\_0 & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize RESERVED \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{36.6 EFUSE\_SEC\_ERR\_FLAG}{anchor584}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{36.6 EFUSE\_SEC\_ERR\_FLAG}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{36.6 EFUSE\_SEC\_ERR\_FLAG} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x4010C044}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize block\_auto\_ch\-eck\_failed & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize The bit indicates block auto check failed after programming. If PGM\_AUTO\_CHK\_EN is set, and controller compared the value read after PGM from the same block, and found the two value not match, this bit will set as an error flag. But if this block is protected, the PGM command in-fact not really send, so this bit will not set. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize shadow\_block\_\-auto\_check\_failed & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize The bit indicates shadow block  auto check failed after programming if double\_bit\_en is set.\newline
If PGM\_AUTO\_CHK\_EN is set, and controller compared the value read after PGM from the same block, and found the two value not match, this bit will set as an error flag. But if this block is protected, the PGM command in-fact not really send, so this bit will not set. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 : 2 & \footnotesize reserved\_2 & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize RESERVED \\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize SEC\_WORD0\_PRO\-T\_FLAG & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize The bit indicates block auto check failed after programming. If PGM\_AUTO\_CHK\_EN is set, and controller compared the value read after PGM from the same block, and found the two value not match, this bit will set as an error flag. But if this block is protected, the PGM command in-fact not really send, so this bit will not set. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 5 & \footnotesize SEC\_WORD1\_PRO\-T\_FLAG & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize The bit indicates shadow block is protected and can not be programmed if double\_bit\_en is set.\newline
If SW send a PGM command to memory block[i], and the controller found this memory block is protected(which means the highest bit is 1), this bit will set to 1. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 : 6 & \footnotesize reserved\_1 & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize RESERVED \\
\hline
\footnotesize 8 & \footnotesize SEC\_PG\_EN\_WR\-\_FLAG & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize the bit indicates write process without setting pg\_en to 1. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 9 & \footnotesize SEC\_VDD\_ON\_R\-D\_FLAG & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize the bit indicates read process without setting vdd\_on to 1. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 10 & \footnotesize SEC\_BLOCK0\_RD\-\_FLAG & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize the bit indicates arbiter read block0 which may indicates unexpected access. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 11 & \footnotesize SEC\_MAGNUM\_WR\-\_FLAG & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize the bit indicates write process without setting magic number. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 12 & \footnotesize SEC\_ENK\_ERR\_\-FLAG & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize the bit enk1 and enk2 is not switch correctly. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 13 & \footnotesize SEC\_ALL0\_CHEC\-K\_FLAG & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize the bit indicates all 0 check fail. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 14 & \footnotesize reserved\_0 & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize RESERVED \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{36.7 EFUSE\_SEC\_FLAG\_CLR}{anchor585}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{36.7 EFUSE\_SEC\_FLAG\_CLR}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{36.7 EFUSE\_SEC\_FLAG\_CLR} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x4010C048}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize SEC\_WORD0\_ERR\-\_CLR & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize write 1 will clear SEC\_WORD0\_ERR\_FLAG. Write 0 will do nothing. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize SEC\_WORD1\_ERR\-\_CLR & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize write 1 will clear SEC\_WORD1\_ERR\_FLAG. Write 0 will do nothing. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 : 2 & \footnotesize reserved\_2 & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize RESERVED \\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize SEC\_WORD0\_PRO\-T\_CLR & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize write 1 will clear SEC\_WORD0\_PROT\_FLAG. Write 0 will do nothing. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 5 & \footnotesize SEC\_WORD1\_PRO\-T\_CLR & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize write 1 will clear SEC\_WORD1\_PROT\_FLAG. Write 0 will do nothing. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 : 6 & \footnotesize reserved\_1 & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize RESERVED \\
\hline
\footnotesize 8 & \footnotesize SEC\_PG\_EN\_WR\-\_CLR & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize write 1 will clear SEC\_PG\_EN\_WR\_FLAG \\
\hline
\footnotesize 9 & \footnotesize SEC\_VDD\_ON\_R\-D\_CLR & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize write 1 will clear SEC\_VDD\_ON\_RD\_FLAG \\
\hline
\footnotesize 10 & \footnotesize SEC\_BLOCK0\_RD\-\_CLR & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize write 1 will clear SEC\_BLOCK0\_RD\_FLAG \\
\hline
\footnotesize 11 & \footnotesize SEC\_MAGNUM\_WR\-\_CLR & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize write 1 will clear SEC\_MAGNUM\_WR\_FLAG \\
\hline
\footnotesize 12 & \footnotesize SEC\_ENK\_ERR\_\-CLR & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize write 1 will clear SEC\_ENK\_ERR\_FLAG \\
\hline
\footnotesize 13 & \footnotesize SEC\_ALL0\_CHEC\-K\_CLR & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize write 1 will clear SEC\_ALL0\_CHECK\_FLAG \\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 14 & \footnotesize reserved\_0 & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize RESERVED \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{36.8 EFUSE\_SEC\_MAGIC\_NUMBER}{anchor586}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{36.8 EFUSE\_SEC\_MAGIC\_NUMBER}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{36.8 EFUSE\_SEC\_MAGIC\_NUMBER} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x4010C04C}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 15 : 0 & \footnotesize SEC\_EFUSE\_MAG\-IC\_NUMBER & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize Magic number, only when this field is 0x8800, the efuse programming command can be handle.\newline
Set the magic number right will lock the power switch and PGM enable.\newline
So if SW want to program efuse memory, except open clocks and power, 2 other conditions must be met :\newline
(1) SEC\_EFUSE\_MAGIC\_NUMBER =0x8800\newline
(2) PG\_EN=1;\newline
(3) Switch the power right; \\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 16 & \footnotesize reserved\_0 & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize RESERVED \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{36.9 EFUSE\_FW\_CFG}{anchor587}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{36.9 EFUSE\_FW\_CFG}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{36.9 EFUSE\_FW\_CFG} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x4010C050}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize CONF\_PROT & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize SEC/NON-SEC address configure. \newline
1: indicates SEC can access EFUSE\_CFG0;\newline
0: indicates NON-SEC can access EFUSE\_CFG0; \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize ACCESS\_PROT & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize 0: SEC access; 1: NON SEC address enable switch \\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 2 & \footnotesize reserved\_0 & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize RESERVED \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{36.10 EFUSE\_PW\_SWT}{anchor588}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{36.10 EFUSE\_PW\_SWT}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{36.10 EFUSE\_PW\_SWT} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x4010C054}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize EFS\_ENK1\_ON & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize VDDQ power switch K2, to safely control this power switch. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize EFS\_ENK2\_ON & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize VDDQ power switch K2, to safely control this power switch. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 2 & \footnotesize NS\_S\_PG\_EN & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize set this bit will open static power supply for efuse memory, before any operation towards to efuse memory this bit have to set to 1. once this bit is cleared, the efuse will go to power down mose. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 & \footnotesize ANLG\_CFG\_EFUS\-E\_SAR\_READ\_MODE & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize read mode, 1'b1: sar mode; 1'b0: normal mode \\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize ANLG\_CFG\_EFUS\-E\_SAR\_LCH\_BIAS\_MODE & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize SAR bias mode \\
\hline
\footnotesize 8 : 5 & \footnotesize ANLG\_CFG\_EFUS\-E\_SAR\_DLY\_BIT & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize SAR delay bits \\
\hline
\footnotesize 11 : 9 & \footnotesize ANLG\_CFG\_EFUS\-E\_SAR\_BIAS\_BIT & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize SAR bias bit control \\
\hline
\footnotesize 16 : 12 & \footnotesize SAR\_READ\_BITS & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize Read bits index for SAR mode \\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 17 & \footnotesize reserved\_0 & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize RESERVED \\
\hline
\end{longtable}

\pdfbookmark[2]{38.13 GPVNDCTL}{anchor668}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{38.13 GPVNDCTL}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{38.13 GPVNDCTL} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40200034}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 : 0 & \footnotesize RegData & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize Register Data (RegData)\newline
Contains the write data for register write.\newline
Read data for register read, valid when VStatus Done is set. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 15 : 8 & \footnotesize VCtrl & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize UTMI+ Vendor Control Register Address (VCtrl) The 4-bit register\newline
address a vendor defined 4-bit parallel output bus. Bits 11:8 of this\newline
field are placed on utmi\_vcontrol[3:0]. ULPI Extended Register\newline
Address (ExtRegAddr) The 6-bit PHY extended register address. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 21 : 16 & \footnotesize RegAddr & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize Register Address (RegAddr)\newline
The 6-bit PHY register address for immediate PHY Register set\newline
access. Set to 6'h2F for Extended PHY Register set access. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 22 & \footnotesize RegWr & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize Register Write (RegWr) Set this bit for register writes, and clear it\newline
for register reads. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 25 & \footnotesize NewRegReq & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize New Register Request (NewRegReq)\newline
The application sets this bit for a new vendor control access.\newline
Values:\newline
0x0 (INACTIVE): New Register Request not active\newline
0x1 (ACTIVE): New Register Request active \\
\hline
\footnotesize 26 & \footnotesize VStsBsy & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize VStatus Busy (VStsBsy)\newline
The core sets this bit when the vendor control access is in progress\newline
and clears this bit when done. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 27 & \footnotesize VStsDone & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize VStatus Done (VStsDone)\newline
The core sets this bit when the vendor control access is done. This\newline
bit is cleared by the core when the application sets the New\newline
Register Request bit (bit 25).\newline
Values:\newline
0x0 (INACTIVE): VStatus Done inactive\newline
0x1 (ACTIVE): VStatus Done active \\
\hline
\end{longtable}

\headinginfo{timer2}
\section{timer2}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{43.1 TIMER0\_CTL}{anchor1199}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{43.1 TIMER0\_CTL}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{43.1 TIMER0\_CTL} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40501000}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize timer0\_en & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  timer0 enable \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize timer0\_reset & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  timer0 reset, active high, cleared automatically.\newline
When one, the Timer Counter is synchronously reset on the next positive edge of PCLK. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 3 & \footnotesize timer0\_tick\_d\-iv\_bypass & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  1, bypass tick divider, TIMER0\_TICK\_DIV register does not exist.\newline
0, tick divider exists. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 7 : 4 & \footnotesize timer0\_tc\_hi\_\-dis & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  1, the High 16bit of TC and MR is ignored when generating match/wrap interrupt.\newline
0, this feature is ignored. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 9 : 8 & \footnotesize timer0\_tick\_f\-req & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  timer0 tick frequency, used to select TC and adjust counter increment value.\newline
2'b00: tick is 64KHz\newline
2'b01: tick is 64KHz\newline
2'b10: tick is 32KHz\newline
2'b11: tick is 16KHz \\
\hline
\footnotesize 12 & \footnotesize timer0\_match0\_\-en & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  1, match0 enable.\newline
0, match0 disable. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 13 & \footnotesize timer0\_match1\_\-en & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  1, match1 enable.\newline
0, match1 disable. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 14 & \footnotesize timer0\_match2\_\-en & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  1, match2 enable.\newline
0, match2 disable. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 15 & \footnotesize timer0\_match3\_\-en & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  1, match3 enable.\newline
0, match3 disable. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{43.2 TIMER0\_TICK\_DIV}{anchor1200}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{43.2 TIMER0\_TICK\_DIV}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{43.2 TIMER0\_TICK\_DIV} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40501004}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 : 0 & \footnotesize timer0\_tick\_d\-iv & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x1 & \footnotesize  timer0 tick div value\newline
2'b00: disable tick\newline
2'b01: tick is div2 from rtc clock\newline
2'b10: tick is div4 from rtc clock\newline
2'b11: tick is div8 from rtc clock \\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize timer0\_tick\_d\-iv\_up & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  timer0 tick div value update, cleared automatically \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{43.3 TIMER0\_INT\_CTRL}{anchor1201}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{43.3 TIMER0\_INT\_CTRL}

\multicolumn{4}{|l}{43.3 TIMER0\_INT\_CTRL} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40501008}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize timer0\_match0\_\-int\_en\_set & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Write: timer0 match0 interrupt enable set\newline
1: Set Enable. An interrupt is generated when MR0 matches the value in the TC.\newline
0: Ignored.\newline
Read: timer0 match0 interrupt enabled\newline
1: Enalbed. An interrupt is generated when MR0 matches the value in the TC.\newline
0: not Enabled. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize timer0\_match1\_\-int\_en\_set & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Write: timer0 match1 interrupt enable set\newline

0: Ignored.\newline

Make sure that this high 16bit configured before the low 16bit. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}

\headinginfo{wdg2}
\section{wdg2}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{50.1 WDG\_LOAD\_VAL}{anchor1376}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{50.1 WDG\_LOAD\_VAL}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{50.1 WDG\_LOAD\_VAL} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40508000}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 0 & \footnotesize wdg\_load\_val & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x3FFFF & \footnotesize  watchdog  timer load value.\newline
In reset mode, software should  load new value before timer decrease to 0.\newline
In interrupt mode, this value  is counting start number.\newline
The default value is about 8  seconds. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{50.2 WDG\_CNT}{anchor1377}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{50.2 WDG\_CNT}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{50.2 WDG\_CNT} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40508004}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 0 & \footnotesize wdg\_cnt & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0xFFFFFFFF & \footnotesize  watchdog counter value \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{50.3 WDG\_CTRL}{anchor1378}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{50.3 WDG\_CTRL}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{50.3 WDG\_CTRL} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40508008}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize wdg\_irq\_en & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Watchdog interrupt enable bit\newline
0: interrupt is disabled\newline
1: interrupt is enabled\newline
For reset mode: wdg\_rst\_en =1,  wdg\_irq\_en=0.\newline
For interrupt mode: wdg\_rst\_en  =0, wdg\_irq\_en=1.\newline
For combined mode: wdg\_rst\_en  =1, wdg\_irq\_en=1. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize wdg\_run & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Watchdog counter open:\newline
0: counter stops.\newline
1: counter runs. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{50.4 WDG\_INT\_CLR}{anchor1379}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{50.4 WDG\_INT\_CLR}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{50.4 WDG\_INT\_CLR} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x4050800C}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize wdg\_int\_clr & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Watchdog interrupt clear\newline
Write 1 to this bit to clear  interrupt\newline
Read this bit always get 0. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{50.5 WDG\_INT\_RAW}{anchor1380}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{50.5 WDG\_INT\_RAW}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{50.5 WDG\_INT\_RAW} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40508010}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize wdg\_int\_raw & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Watchdog interrupt raw status. Watch  dog reset cannot clear this raw status. Write wdg\_int\_clr can clear this raw  status. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize wdg\_ld\_busy & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Watchdog  load busy status\newline
0:  Watchdog is ready for new loading\newline
1: Last  loading is not completed\newline
Software  must not load new value when this bit is busy, that is, this bit should be  checked before any new loading.\newline
This  bit is set after a new loading, and lasts two or three RTC clock cycles,  about 60us - 92us. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{50.6 WDG\_INT\_MASK}{anchor1381}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{50.6 WDG\_INT\_MASK}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{50.6 WDG\_INT\_MASK} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40508014}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize wdg\_int\_mask & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Watchdog interrupt masked  status \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{50.7 WDG\_LOCK}{anchor1382}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{50.7 WDG\_LOCK}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{50.7 WDG\_LOCK} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x40508C00}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 15 : 0 & \footnotesize wdg\_lock & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Watchdog lock control\newline
Write 32'h1ACCE551 to this register to unlock  watchdog.\newline
Write other value to this  register to lock watchdog\newline
If reading this register, bit-0  is lock status, and other bits are reserved.\newline
If watchdog is locked, all  control registers cannot be written by software. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}

\headinginfo{wdg4}
\section{wdg4}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{56.1 WDG\_LOAD\_VAL}{anchor1650}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{56.1 WDG\_LOAD\_VAL}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{56.1 WDG\_LOAD\_VAL} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x70001400}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 0 & \footnotesize wdg\_load\_val & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x3FFFF & \footnotesize  watchdog  timer load value.\newline
In reset mode, software should  load new value before timer decrease to 0.\newline
In interrupt mode, this value  is counting start number.\newline
The default value is about 8  seconds. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{56.2 WDG\_CNT}{anchor1651}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{56.2 WDG\_CNT}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{56.2 WDG\_CNT} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x70001404}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 31 : 0 & \footnotesize wdg\_cnt & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0xFFFFFFFF & \footnotesize  watchdog counter value \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{56.3 WDG\_CTRL}{anchor1652}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{56.3 WDG\_CTRL}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{56.3 WDG\_CTRL} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x70001408}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize wdg\_irq\_en & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Watchdog interrupt enable bit\newline
0: interrupt is disabled\newline
1: interrupt is enabled\newline
For reset mode: wdg\_rst\_en =1,  wdg\_irq\_en=0.\newline
For interrupt mode: wdg\_rst\_en  =0, wdg\_irq\_en=1.\newline
For combined mode: wdg\_rst\_en  =1, wdg\_irq\_en=1. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 1 & \footnotesize wdg\_run & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Watchdog counter open:\newline
0: counter stops.\newline
1: counter runs. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{56.4 WDG\_INT\_CLR}{anchor1653}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{56.4 WDG\_INT\_CLR}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{56.4 WDG\_INT\_CLR} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x7000140C}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize wdg\_int\_clr & \footnotesize wo & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Watchdog interrupt clear\newline
Write 1 to this bit to clear  interrupt\newline
Read this bit always get 0. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{56.5 WDG\_INT\_RAW}{anchor1654}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{56.5 WDG\_INT\_RAW}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{56.5 WDG\_INT\_RAW} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x70001410}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize wdg\_int\_raw & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Watchdog interrupt raw status. Watch  dog reset cannot clear this raw status. Write wdg\_int\_clr can clear this raw  status. \\
\hline
\footnotesize 4 & \footnotesize wdg\_ld\_busy & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Watchdog  load busy status\newline
0:  Watchdog is ready for new loading\newline
1: Last  loading is not completed\newline
Software  must not load new value when this bit is busy, that is, this bit should be  checked before any new loading.\newline
This  bit is set after a new loading, and lasts two or three RTC clock cycles,  about 60us - 92us. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{56.6 WDG\_INT\_MASK}{anchor1655}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{56.6 WDG\_INT\_MASK}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{56.6 WDG\_INT\_MASK} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x70001414}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 0 & \footnotesize wdg\_int\_mask & \footnotesize ro & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Watchdog interrupt masked  status \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
        
\pdfbookmark[2]{56.7 WDG\_LOCK}{anchor1656}
\addcontentsline{lot}{table}{56.7 WDG\_LOCK}
\begin{longtable}{|p{0.1\linewidth}|p{0.25\linewidth}|p{0.05\linewidth}|p{0.15\linewidth}|p{0.45\linewidth}|}
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{|l}{56.7 WDG\_LOCK} & \multicolumn{1}{r|}{0x70002000}\\
\hline
\rowcolor[gray]{.8} Bits & Field Name & Acc & Default & Description\\
\hline
\footnotesize 15 : 0 & \footnotesize wdg\_lock & \footnotesize rw & \footnotesize 0x0 & \footnotesize  Watchdog lock control\newline
Write 32'h1ACCE551 to this register to unlock  watchdog.\newline
Write other value to this  register to lock watchdog\newline
If reading this register, bit-0  is lock status, and other bits are reserved.\newline
If watchdog is locked, all  control registers cannot be written by software. \\
\hline
\end{longtable}
